commands.review_summary:
The PR switches the stable gateway to the published npm shrinkwrap path when available, keeps source fallback, restructures CI/check outputs, updates pin automation/docs, and adds maintainer CI metering.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR is a packaging/default-build and CI-gate change, not a reported runtime bug. The review is based on current main source, the PR diff, GitHub state, and proof/check metadata.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human maintainers need to accept the provenance, security, CI-gate, and dirty-branch decisions; there is no narrow automated repair target.

target.title: Default gateway to npm shrinkwrap
target.body:
for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels

# Human written summary:
The intent of this change is, as written by a human:
> clean elegant install in nix of npm package; we have many hacks from dirty packaging, we want to clean them up as much as possible to reduce total build time (both in CI and locally) and make packaging less fragile and more predictable; using tools like shrinkwrap if we can; and other optimizations as necessary. fewer dependencies in the tree is also good, faster build time is good, smaller is good, etc, you know the drill.

_The rest of this PR was written by GPT-5 Codex, running in the Codex desktop harness._

# Changes
- Builds the stable `openclaw-gateway` from the published `openclaw` npm package when the stable source pin provides `gatewayNpmDepsHash` and the bundled ACPX package is available.
- Keeps source/pnpm builds as the explicit fallback for `gatewaySrc` and dogfood-style source pins.
- Uses the upstream npm shrinkwrap path for the default gateway dependency graph and patches the published dist for Nix-store runtime discovery.
- Reuses PR #99's generated runtime-plugin package machinery for the gateway-local ACPX bridge instead of keeping a private ACPX npm wrapper.
- Narrows the default `ci` aggregate to the default install/config/apply contract while keeping QMD, broad runtime-plugin, and plugin-instance proof as explicit check outputs.
- Adds `maintainers/performance-audit.md` as the commit-keyed performance ledger, including a current `origin/main` vs PR snapshot.
- Adds CI metering for Nix copy/build/eval behavior, build-closure hotspots, and Linux HM activation timing.

# Why
Upstream `openclaw@2026.6.1` publishes `npm-shrinkwrap.json`. npm documents shrinkwrap as the publishable lockfile form, and nixpkgs documents `buildNpmPackage`/`npmDepsHash` as the fixed-output npm dependency cache path. This PR uses that upstream package shape for the default stable gateway instead of rebuilding the full source/pnpm tree every time.

Docs checked:
- https://nixos.org/manual/nixpkgs/stable/#javascript
- https://docs.npmjs.com/files/package-lock.json/
- https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v11/commands/npm-shrinkwrap

# Measured Impact
Current snapshot compares `origin/main` at `6bd88e582c44b689cc1bf413d294197944a42c4b` with PR head metrics measured at `d3e63129c6670fda5fbd4d764d8c22490313ad5f`. Latest pushed head `85c0cee4f3a4a54cb84852482942bc6d1e556f3f` is docs-only and revalidated by CI.

| Metric | `origin/main` | Current PR | Change |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| `openclaw-gateway` closure | 2,273,877,888 B | 904,981,328 B | 60.2% smaller |
| `openclaw` closure | 3,215,431,032 B | 1,846,534,464 B | 42.6% smaller |
| gateway output size | 2,169,012,224 B | 339,697,664 B | 84.3% smaller |
| package manifests | 1,452 | 541 | 62.7% fewer |
| files under `lib/openclaw` | 97,909 | 32,840 | 66.5% fewer |
| Darwin `ci` direct input drvs | 41 | 11 | 73.2% fewer |
| Linux `ci` direct input drvs | 43 | 11 | 74.4% fewer |
| direct runtime plugin package inputs in `ci` | 28 per system | 0 per system | removed from default gate |
| Darwin `default-instance` closure paths | 2,879 | 863 | 70.0% fewer |
| Linux `default-instance` closure paths | 3,695 | 357 | 90.3% fewer |
| Garnix include targets | 10 | 5 | 50.0% fewer |
| gateway forced rebuild | 399.37s then Nix determinism failure | 56.27s success | deterministic npm path |

Remote final head proof:
- GitHub Actions run `27059620425`: Linux `2m06s`, macOS `1m56s`, success.
- Linux aggregate: `113s`, `923` fetched paths, `932 MiB` download, `4.2 GiB` unpacked, `28` built derivations.
- macOS aggregate: `74s`, `226` fetched paths, `327 MiB` download, `1.8 GiB` unpacked, `0` built derivations.
- Garnix: green on flake eval, Darwin `ci`, and stable `openclaw` / `openclaw-gateway` packages on Darwin and Linux.
- Socket: green check status, with dependency warnings still requiring maintainer acceptance.

# Proof
Local and remote proof recorded in `maintainers/performance-audit.md` includes:

- `git diff --check`
- `nix eval --accept-flake-config --json .#checks.<system> --apply 'attrs: builtins.attrNames attrs'`
- `RUNNER_TEMP=/tmp NIX_METER_BUILD_CLOSURE=0 scripts/ci-nix-build.sh ... .#checks.<system>.ci`
- `RUNNER_TEMP=/tmp NIX_METER_BUILD_CLOSURE=0 scripts/ci-nix-build.sh ... .#checks.<system>.plugin-instance`
- `/usr/bin/time -p nix build --accept-flake-config --rebuild --no-link --print-out-paths git+file://$PWD?rev=<head>#packages.aarch64-darwin.openclaw-gateway`
- `scripts/summarize-nix-build-log.mjs --github-log /tmp/nix-openclaw-ci-logs/run-27059620425.log`

# Plugin PR / Stacking
PR #100 is stacked on PR #99 (`codex/runtime-plugin-shrinkwrap-materialization`). Runtime plugins are intentionally not solved here. This PR preserves the public `programs.openclaw.runtimePlugins` interface and keeps broad plugin proof explicit rather than in the default `ci` aggregate.

The remaining ACPX bridge is gateway-local because the published `openclaw@2026.6.1` npm package does not include ACPX runtime files. Once PR #99 lands, the next review should decide whether that bridge can be retired cleanly.

# Risks
- The default stable gateway artifact changes from source-built output to the published npm package, so maintainers need to accept that default package/source boundary.
- Socket dependency warnings remain a maintainer supply-chain decision; this PR does not suppress them.
- Default `ci` is intentionally narrower than current main. Optional QMD, broad plugin catalog, and plugin-instance proof remain explicit checks, but not all are in the default aggregate.
- Garnix is still used as a substituter/cache in the current workflow. The audit records what comes from Garnix so a future PR can replace that dependency deliberately.

# Follow-ups
- After PR #99 lands, re-check whether the gateway-local ACPX bridge can be deleted.
- Consider splitting the CI/performance metering and check-graph simplification into a follow-on stacked PR if reviewers want #100 to contain only the npm-default package flip.
- If CI speed is still the north star, benchmark larger GitHub Actions runners and non-Garnix cache layers in a separate PR; do not fold provider migration into this package-default change.
target.author: joshp123
